narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD structural weakness because DXY is down 0.33% with USD CSM at zero and H4 signals across multiple pairs confirm broad dollar selling pressure
2. CHF and NZD currency strength because CHF ranks 100 and NZD ranks 99 on D1 CSM, with USDCHF generating a confirmed H4 sell signal aligned with negative EURUSD-USDCHF correlation
3. Copper-led risk appetite because copper is up 2.89% and S&P 500 is up 0.82%, together delivering the macro overlay risk-on confirmation that overrides the news-flow risk-off label

PRICE CONFIRMS

EURUSD H4 buy signal at plus 4 is corroborated by EUR accelerating sharply on H4 CSM at 94 versus only 39 on D1, confirming short-term momentum rather than a sustained reversal, with GBPUSD following at correlation 0.94 and also accelerating on H4 CSM. USDCHF sell at minus 4 on H4 directly mirrors the EURUSD buy and the negative 0.78 correlation between those pairs holds tightly, making CHF the leading vehicle. NZDUSD shows D1 CSM fading from 99 to 67 on H4, flagging it as a laggard relative to EUR and CHF despite high headline CSM strength.

REGIME

Mixed, conflicted. VIX is up 1.23% to 17.29 which signals residual caution, but S&P 500 is up 0.82% and copper is surging 2.89%, and Gold at 4716 is only marginally higher, consistent with a risk-on lean that is not yet a clean directional conviction.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Risk sentiment label versus price behavior: news flags risk-off sentiment but copper up 2.89%, equities up, and DXY falling collectively reject that narrative on price evidence
2. NZD D1 versus H4 CSM divergence: NZD ranks highest at 99 on D1 but is fading to 67 on H4 while EUR and GBP are accelerating, creating a currency rotation conflict within the risk-on theme

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF down because CHF is strongest currency, USD is weakest, H4 sell confirmed, DXY falling - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: EURUSD up because EUR accelerating on H4 CSM, H4 buy signal at plus 4, USD at zero CSM - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: AUDUSD because NZD and AUD CSM fading on H4 while D1 remains elevated creating directional ambiguity
Watch: DXY breaking below 97.70 confirms USD selling acceleration and validates both primary and secondary trades
key_observation: Strong US nonfarm payrolls beat expectations significantly but USD weakened amid Middle East tensions and Iran military threats. Geopolitical risk premium offsetting positive labor data. Canadian labor market deteriorated sharply, supporting BoC on-hold stance. European weakness evident with industrial slump and equities closing lower. Sentiment remains fragile despite solid US employment.
watch: Strait of Hormuz escalation, Iran military response trajectory, US-Iran diplomatic developments, University of Michigan sentiment revision expectations, equity market stabilization, energy price volatility transmission to currencies, ECB inflation guidance, BoC rate cut probability shift
